levels.label: Explorer
levels.copy: The Entrepreneurial Competence Compass pattern is still mostly informal or ad hoc. Pick one stabilising action, document it, and avoid adding a long process before the basics are real.

levels.label: Initiator
levels.copy: Some Entrepreneurial Competence Compass practices exist, but they still depend on individual memory and effort. The next step is two repeatable routines with clear owners.

levels.label: Value Builder
levels.copy: Core Entrepreneurial Competence Compass practices are repeatable, but evidence quality and ownership are uneven. Work on the weakest dimension before optimising the strongest one.

levels.label: Entrepreneurial Operator
levels.copy: Entrepreneurial Competence Compass practices are measured and connected to decisions. The main work is now removing the constraint that prevents the system from scaling cleanly.

levels.label: Ecosystem Creator
levels.copy: Entrepreneurial Competence Compass is operating as a learning system. Keep calibrating against evidence, stress-test resilience, and avoid treating a strong score as a permanent guarantee.

recommendations.opportunity-and-vision: Keep an opportunity log and validate one recurring problem through five interviews each month.
recommendations.resource-mobilisation: Map the next milestone capabilities and acquire one missing resource through learning, partnership, or recruitment.
recommendations.action-and-experimentation: Turn the largest uncertainty into a reversible experiment with a precommitted decision rule.
recommendations.ethics-and-shared-value: Create a stakeholder map and add one harm-prevention and one shared-value metric to the initiative.

defaultSecondAction: Retake the diagnostic after a focused 30-day improvement cycle and compare only your own progress unless a team comparison is explicitly agreed.
reflectionQuestion: Which assumption behind your weakest dimension deserves the fastest evidence check?
methodologyNote: The current version uses four dimensions with two questions each. Dimension scores are normalised to 0-100. The balanced index weights 70% average dimension performance and 30% the weakest dimension. No percentile or benchmark is shown until a compatible sample exists.
shareNote: Share cards reveal only the level and strongest area. They do not publish individual answers, sensitive weaknesses, legal or financial details, security gaps, or family-specific information.
